meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Scaling Projects with Antigravity Template
content.seo_description: Stop manual coding hell. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to scale your freelance business with automated, high-speed workflows.
content.answer_block: The bottleneck in your freelance business is manual boilerplate. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you standardize your architecture, enabling Claude Code to scaffold entire features in seconds. This shift from manual coding to intelligent orchestration allows you to scale output, reduce technical debt, and triple your project delivery speed.
content.intro:
You’re trapped in a cycle of 'pixel-perfect' manual labor, reinventing the wheel for every client while your profit margins evaporate. The industry calls it craftsmanship, but we know it’s a scalability death trap. When every project starts from a blank `index.html`, you aren't a business owner; you're a high-priced laborer.

Relief arrives through the Antigravity project template. By decoupling your business logic from architectural boilerplate, you create a repeatable, high-velocity engine. We’re combining this with Claude Code to turn your terminal into an autonomous factory. It’s time to stop coding and start shipping.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffolding
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure all future features are predictable and modular.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard architecture with support for modular feature folders and shared utility hooks.

steps.title: Configure Domain-Driven Service Layers
steps.description: Abstract your business logic into services to prevent the 'spaghetti code' that kills scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a service-layer abstraction for the current project that separates API communication from UI state management.

steps.title: Automate Type-Safe Data Contracts
steps.description: Force type safety across your stack to eliminate runtime bugs during rapid iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Zod schemas and TypeScript interfaces for all data entities defined in the Antigravity template.

steps.title: Inject Feature-Specific Controllers
steps.description: Use the template to generate controllers that hook directly into the existing service layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new feature controller for user authentication that follows the established Antigravity service patterns.

steps.title: Execute Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Ensure your scaled architecture remains stable by automating tests against the templates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for all newly created feature controllers, ensuring 90% coverage.

pro_tips: Always use a custom .claudeignore file to prevent the AI from indexing your bloated node_modules or build artifacts.
Tag your Antigravity components with custom metadata comments so Claude Code can identify them for mass refactoring later.
Treat your project template as a living repository; commit every architectural improvement to your personal 'Master Template' repo.
Use Claude Code's '--watch' flag during development to allow the AI to catch dependency errors as soon as you save a file.
Never manually write boilerplate; if you find yourself typing a repetitive pattern more than twice, encode it into an Antigravity snippet.
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, focus on building a library of reusable 'micro-features' that you can drag and drop into any new client project using Claude Code.
content.conversion_block: Stop trading hours for dollars and start building an automated agency engine that handles the heavy lifting for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
